raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint against American Express for repeated failure to conduct a reasonable investigation into a disputed transaction, as required by the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ). 
On XX/XX/year>, I disputed a charge of {$3500.00} from XXXX because the accommodations were uninhabitable when I arrived at the location. For example, there was mold present visually and by smell throughout the apartment, an exterior door was not secure, and the apartment was excessively dirty, including smudges, stains, dirt, grime, sand, blood, XXXX, soap, etc., in all areas. Despite providing comprehensive documentation, including photos, videos, and correspondence to XXXX, they refused to uphold their own Rebooking and refunding for homes policy, Ground rules for home hosts policy, and Safety requirements for homes guidelines.

I disputed the charge with American Express, and they subsequently closed the dispute, stating, A partial refund was already provided by XXXX for the issue. This was not the case ; the partial refund I received previously from XXXX was for a previous issue where the host could not accommodate all the dates I originally reserved. I called American Express on XX/XX/XXXX and re-submitted the dispute, explaining the reason for the previous refund. The American Express associate noted with the dispute all the facts given by me for review. American Express closed the dispute in less than 24 hours without reviewing my files or the notes from the associate, and stated the same reason for the refusal. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, I spoke with a representative who admitted on a recorded line that my documentation had not been properly reviewed. My account was allegedly put on a " back-office hold '' for a manual review, and I was promised a follow-up call. I never received a call, and the dispute was closed for a third time.

American Express has demonstrated a systemic failure to follow federal dispute resolution procedures. They have acknowledged their own internal error but have refused to correct it or review the evidence that proves this charge is a billing error.
